svg.aria-label: React — for data engineering. The CocoIndex mental model: Target = F(Source). A persistent-state-driven dataflow where you declare the desired target state and the engine keeps it in sync with the latest source data and code, forever, with minimum cost. Source files flow through your Python transformation F into the target index; only the Δ (delta) is reprocessed, and every row in the target traces back to its exact source bytes. Four core properties: Python (not a DAG), declare target state, lineage end-to-end, and incremental at any scale. Keywords: React for data engineering, declarative ETL, persistent state, data lineage, dataflow, Δ only, incremental indexing, CocoIndex.
svg.title: React — for data engineering · Target = F(Source) (light theme)
svg.desc: Light-theme version of the CocoIndex mental-model hero: at the top a big formula 'Target = F(Source)' in the brand warm gradient, a persistent-state tagline, a middle band animating source files flowing through the coconut engine into a live target index (Δ-only), and a bottom row of four coloured property cards — Python not a DAG (indigo), declare target state (amber), lineage end-to-end (brand coral), incremental at any scale (emerald). Recolored for white GitHub README backgrounds — every token clears WCAG AA on #ffffff.
svg.text: Declare the target. The engine keeps it in sync with the latest source & code — forever, for the Δ only.

g.text: ✓ IN SYNC · EVERY DOT → SOURCE BYTE
You say what the index contains.

svg.text: Your code is as simple as the one-off version — the engine does the rest.
